data.Name: AarSvc_14814b
data.Description: Runtime for activating conversational agent applications

data.Name: AxInstSV
data.Description: Provides User Account Control validation for the installation of ActiveX controls from the Internet and enables management of ActiveX control installation based on Group Policy settings. This service is started on demand and if disabled the installation of ActiveX controls will behave according to default browser settings.

data.Name: AdobeARMservice
data.Description: Adobe Acrobat Updater keeps your Adobe software up to date.

data.Name: ADPSvc
data.Description: This service is used for Aggregated Data Platform scenarios.

data.Name: APC Data Service
data.Description: PowerChute Personal Edition service for managing data operations.

data.Name: APC UPS Service
data.Description: PowerChute Personal Edition service for managing battery backup power events.

data.Name: AppReadiness
data.Description: Gets apps ready for use the first time a user signs in to this PC and when adding new apps.

data.Name: AppIDSvc
data.Description: Determines and verifies the identity of an application. Disabling this service will prevent AppLocker from being enforced.

data.Name: Appinfo
data.Description: Facilitates the running of interactive applications with additional administrative privileges.  If this service is stopped, users will be unable to launch applications with the additional administrative privileges they may require to perform desired user tasks.

data.Name: ALG
data.Description: Provides support for 3rd party protocol plug-ins for Internet Connection Sharing

data.Name: AppMgmt
data.Description: Processes installation, removal, and enumeration requests for software deployed through Group Policy. If the service is disabled, users will be unable to install, remove, or enumerate software deployed through Group Policy. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: appprotectionsvc
data.Description: AppProtection software is designed to safeguard entry fields and prevent keylogging spyware from accessing password details and other sensitive information from users.

data.Name: AppXSvc
data.Description: Provides infrastructure support for deploying Store applications. This service is started on demand and if disabled Store applications will not be deployed to the system, and may not function properly.

data.Name: AssignedAccessManagerSvc
data.Description: AssignedAccessManager Service supports kiosk experience in Windows.

data.Name: tzautoupdate
data.Description: Automatically sets the system time zone.

data.Name: BthAvctpSvc
data.Description: This is Audio Video Control Transport Protocol service

data.Name: BITS
data.Description: Transfers files in the background using idle network bandwidth. If the service is disabled, then any applications that depend on BITS, such as Windows Update or MSN Explorer, will be unable to automatically download programs and other information.

info.what: Downloads Windows updates in the background using idle bandwidth.

data.Name: BrokerInfrastructure
data.Description: Windows infrastructure service that controls which background tasks can run on the system.

data.Name: BFE
data.Description: The Base Filtering Engine (BFE) is a service that manages firewall and Internet Protocol security (IPsec) policies and implements user mode filtering. Stopping or disabling the BFE service will significantly reduce the security of the system. It will also result in unpredictable behavior in IPsec management and firewall applications.

data.Name: BcastDVRUserService_14814b
data.Description: This user service is used for Game Recordings and Live Broadcasts

data.Name: BDESVC
data.Description: BDESVC hosts the BitLocker Drive Encryption service. BitLocker Drive Encryption provides secure startup for the operating system, as well as full volume encryption for OS, fixed or removable volumes. This service allows BitLocker to prompt users for various actions related to their volumes when mounted, and unlocks volumes automatically without user interaction. Additionally, it stores recovery information to Active Directory, if available, and, if necessary, ensures the most recent recovery certificates are used.  Stopping or disabling the service would prevent users from leveraging this functionality.

data.Name: wbengine
data.Description: The WBENGINE service is used by Windows Backup to perform backup and recovery operations. If this service is stopped by a user, it may cause the currently running backup or recovery operation to fail. Disabling this service may disable backup and recovery operations using Windows Backup on this computer.

data.Name: BTAGService
data.Description: Service supporting the audio gateway role of the Bluetooth Handsfree Profile.

data.Name: bthserv
data.Description: The Bluetooth service supports discovery and association of remote Bluetooth devices.  Stopping or disabling this service may cause already installed Bluetooth devices to fail to operate properly and prevent new devices from being discovered or associated.

data.Name: BluetoothUserService_14814b
data.Description: The Bluetooth user service supports proper functionality of Bluetooth features relevant to each user session.

data.Name: PeerDistSvc
data.Description: This service caches network content from peers on the local subnet.

data.Name: camsvc
data.Description: Provides facilities for managing UWP apps access to app capabilities as well as checking an app's access to specific app capabilities

data.Name: CaptureService_14814b
data.Description: Enables optional screen capture functionality for applications that call the Windows.Graphics.Capture API.

data.Name: cbdhsvc_14814b
data.Description: This user service is used for Clipboard scenarios

data.Name: CDPUserSvc_14814b
data.Description: This user service is used for Connected Devices Platform scenarios

data.Name: autotimesvc
data.Description: This service sets time based on NITZ messages from a Mobile Network

data.Name: CertPropSvc
data.Description: Copies user certificates and root certificates from smart cards into the current user's certificate store, detects when a smart card is inserted into a smart card reader, and, if needed, installs the smart card Plug and Play minidriver.

data.Name: CtxPkm
data.Description: Citrix App Data Protection Service

data.Name: CtxsDPS
data.Description: Citrix Device Posture Service

data.Name: CtxAdpPolicy
data.Description: Citrix Policy Service

data.Name: CWAUpdaterService
data.Description: Keeps your Citrix Workspace app up to date and supports features that require administrative privileges. If you disable or stop this service, any feature that depends on it will fail. This service uninstalls itself when you uninstall Citrix Workspace app.

data.Name: CoworkVMService
data.Description: Desktop application for Claude.ai

data.Name: ClipSVC
data.Description: Provides infrastructure support for the Microsoft Store. This service is started on demand and if disabled applications bought using the Microsoft Store will not behave correctly.

data.Name: CloudBackupRestoreSvc_14814b
data.Description: Monitors the system for changes in application and setting states and performs cloud backup and restore operations when required.

data.Name: KeyIso
data.Description: The CNG key isolation service is hosted in the LSA process. The service provides key process isolation to private keys and associated cryptographic operations as required by the Common Criteria. The service stores and uses long-lived keys in a secure process complying with Common Criteria requirements.

data.Name: EventSystem
data.Description: Supports System Event Notification Service (SENS), which provides automatic distribution of events to subscribing Component Object Model (COM) components. If the service is stopped, SENS will close and will not be able to provide logon and logoff notifications. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: COMSysApp
data.Description: Manages the configuration and tracking of Component Object Model (COM)+-based components. If the service is stopped, most COM+-based components will not function properly. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: CDPSvc
data.Description: This service is used for Connected Devices Platform scenarios

data.Name: DiagTrack
data.Description: The Connected User Experiences and Telemetry service enables features that support in-application and connected user experiences. Additionally, this service manages the event driven collection and transmission of diagnostic and usage information (used to improve the experience and quality of the Windows Platform) when the diagnostics and usage privacy option settings are enabled under Feedback and Diagnostics.

info.what: Sends usage and diagnostic data to Microsoft. Safe to disable for privacy.

data.Name: ConsentUxUserSvc_14814b
data.Description: Allows the system to request user consent to allow apps to access sensitive resources and information such as the device's location

data.Name: CoreMessagingRegistrar
data.Description: Manages communication between system components.

data.Name: VaultSvc
data.Description: Provides secure storage and retrieval of credentials to users, applications and security service packages.

data.Name: CredentialEnrollmentManagerUserSvc_14814b
data.Description: Credential Enrollment Manager

data.Name: CryptSvc
data.Description: Provides three management services: Catalog Database Service, which confirms the signatures of Windows files and allows new programs to be installed; Protected Root Service, which adds and removes Trusted Root Certification Authority certificates from this computer; and Automatic Root Certificate Update Service, which retrieves root certificates from Windows Update and enable scenarios such as SSL. If this service is stopped, these management services will not function properly. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Manages certificates and crypto operations. Required for Windows Update and TLS.

data.Name: DsSvc
data.Description: Provides data brokering between applications.

data.Name: DusmSvc
data.Description: Network data usage, data limit, restrict background data, metered networks.

data.Name: DcomLaunch
data.Description: The DCOMLAUNCH service launches COM and DCOM servers in response to object activation requests. If this service is stopped or disabled, programs using COM or DCOM will not function properly. It is strongly recommended that you have the DCOMLAUNCH service running.

data.Name: dcsvc
data.Description: Process Declared Configuration documents recevied from MDM and other channels and perform configurations on device

data.Name: DoSvc
data.Description: Performs content delivery optimization tasks

data.Name: DellClientManagementService
data.Description: Enables Dell applications to manage Dell specific features of the system. If this service is stopped, any services that explicitly depend on it will cause this service to be restarted. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to function.

data.Name: SupportAssistAgent
data.Description: Dell SupportAssist keeps your PC up to date and running at its best with recommended software and driver updates. It also helps resolve issues quickly by detecting common problems and sending issue details to Dell Technical Support agents on your behalf.

data.Name: Dell SupportAssist Remediation
data.Description: Dell SupportAssist Remediation Service

data.Name: DellTechHub
data.Description: Foundational service managing Dell applications through Dell TechHub

data.Name: DeviceAssociationService
data.Description: Enables pairing between the system and wired or wireless devices.

data.Name: DeviceInstall
data.Description: Enables a computer to recognize and adapt to hardware changes with little or no user input. Stopping or disabling this service will result in system instability.

data.Name: DmEnrollmentSvc
data.Description: Performs Device Enrollment Activities for Device Management

data.Name: dmwappushservice
data.DisplayName: Device Management Wireless Application Protocol (WAP) Push message Routing Service
data.Description: Routes Wireless Application Protocol (WAP) Push messages received by the device and synchronizes Device Management sessions

data.Name: DsmSvc
data.Description: Enables the detection, download and installation of device-related software. If this service is disabled, devices may be configured with outdated software, and may not work correctly.

data.Name: DeviceAssociationBrokerSvc_14814b
data.Description: Enables apps to pair devices

data.Name: DevicePickerUserSvc_14814b
data.Description: This user service is used for managing the Miracast, DLNA, and DIAL UI

data.Name: DevicesFlowUserSvc_14814b
data.Description: Allows ConnectUX and PC Settings to Connect and Pair with WiFi displays and Bluetooth devices.

data.Name: DevQueryBroker
data.Description: Enables apps to discover devices with a backgroud task

data.Name: Dhcp
data.Description: Registers and updates IP addresses and DNS records for this computer. If this service is stopped, this computer will not receive dynamic IP addresses and DNS updates. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Gets your IP address from the router. Disabling breaks network connectivity.

data.Name: diagsvc
data.Description: Executes diagnostic actions for troubleshooting support

data.Name: DPS
data.Description: The Diagnostic Policy Service enables problem detection, troubleshooting and resolution for Windows components.  If this service is stopped, diagnostics will no longer function.

data.Name: WdiServiceHost
data.Description: The Diagnostic Service Host is used by the Diagnostic Policy Service to host diagnostics that need to run in a Local Service context.  If this service is stopped, any diagnostics that depend on it will no longer function.

data.Name: WdiSystemHost
data.Description: The Diagnostic System Host is used by the Diagnostic Policy Service to host diagnostics that need to run in a Local System context.  If this service is stopped, any diagnostics that depend on it will no longer function.

data.Name: DialogBlockingService
data.Description: Dialog Blocking Service

data.Name: DisplayEnhancementService
data.Description: A service for managing display enhancement such as brightness control.

data.Name: DispBrokerDesktopSvc
data.Description: Manages the connection and configuration of local and remote displays

data.Name: TrkWks
data.Description: Maintains links between NTFS files within a computer or across computers in a network.

data.Name: MSDTC
data.Description: Coordinates transactions that span multiple resource managers, such as databases, message queues, and file systems. If this service is stopped, these transactions will fail. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: Dnscache
data.Description: The DNS Client service (dnscache) sends Domain Name System (DNS) queries, caches query results, and registers the computer's full name on the network. This service is not stoppable.

info.what: Caches DNS lookups to speed up web browsing. Rarely worth disabling.

data.Name: MapsBroker
data.Description: Windows service for application access to downloaded maps. This service is started on-demand by application accessing downloaded maps. Disabling this service will prevent apps from accessing maps.

data.Name: embeddedmode
data.Description: The Embedded Mode service enables scenarios related to Background Applications.  Disabling this service will prevent Background Applications from being activated.

data.Name: EFS
data.Description: Provides the core file encryption technology used to store encrypted files on NTFS file system volumes. If this service is stopped or disabled, applications will be unable to access encrypted files.

data.Name: EntAppSvc
data.Description: Enables enterprise application management.

data.Name: ExpressVPN App Service
data.Description: This service ensures that VPN location configuration settings are updated regularly. If this service is stopped, ExpressVPN will not be able to connect or disconnect from the VPN.

data.Name: ExpressVPN System Service
data.Description: This service enables ExpressVPN to update Windows firewall rules. If this service is stopped, ExpressVPN will not function.

data.Name: ExpressVPN VPN Service
data.Description: This service enables ExpressVPN to connect to VPN locations. If this service is stopped, ExpressVPN cannot function, and all VPN connections will be terminated.

data.Name: EapHost
data.Description: The Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP) service provides network authentication in such scenarios as 802.1x wired and wireless, VPN, and Network Access Protection (NAP).  EAP also provides application programming interfaces (APIs) that are used by network access clients, including wireless and VPN clients, during the authentication process.  If you disable this service, this computer is prevented from accessing networks that require EAP authentication.

data.Name: Fax
data.Description: Enables you to send and receive faxes, utilizing fax resources available on this computer or on the network.

info.what: Fax support. Almost certainly unused. Safe to disable.

data.Name: fhsvc
data.Description: Protects user files from accidental loss by copying them to a backup location

data.Name: FileSyncHelper
data.Description: Helper service for OneDrive

data.Name: fdPHost
data.Description: The FDPHOST service hosts the Function Discovery (FD) network discovery providers. These FD providers supply network discovery services for the Simple Services Discovery Protocol (SSDP) and Web Services - Discovery (WS-D) protocol. Stopping or disabling the FDPHOST service will disable network discovery for these protocols when using FD. When this service is unavailable, network services using FD and relying on these discovery protocols will be unable to find network devices or resources.

data.Name: FDResPub
data.Description: Publishes this computer and resources attached to this computer so they can be discovered over the network.  If this service is stopped, network resources will no longer be published and they will not be discovered by other computers on the network.

data.Name: GameInputSvc
data.Description: Enables keyboards, mice, gamepads, and other input devices to be used with the GameInput API.

data.Name: lfsvc
data.Description: This service monitors the current location of the system and manages geofences (a geographical location with associated events).  If you turn off this service, applications will be unable to use or receive notifications for geolocation or geofences.

data.Name: GoogleChromeElevationService
data.Description: Provides encryption services and a secure way for recovering Google Chrome if it gets out of date. If this service is disabled, Google Chrome may lose access to encrypted data, and Google Chrome may not be able recover itself.

data.Name: GoogleUpdaterInternalService148.0.7730.0
data.Description: Keeps your Google software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your Google software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no Google software using it.

data.Name: GoogleUpdaterService148.0.7730.0
data.Description: Keeps your Google software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your Google software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no Google software using it.

data.Name: GraphicsPerfSvc
data.Description: Graphics performance monitor service

data.Name: gpsvc
data.Description: The service is responsible for applying settings configured by administrators for the computer and users through the Group Policy component. If the service is disabled, the settings will not be applied and applications and components will not be manageable through Group Policy. Any components or applications that depend on the Group Policy component might not be functional if the service is disabled.

data.Name: hns
data.Description: Provides support for Windows Virtual Networks.

data.Name: hpatchmon
data.Description: Monitors Hotpatch ETW Trace providers and reports failures.

data.Name: hidserv
data.Description: Activates and maintains the use of hot buttons on keyboards, remote controls, and other multimedia devices. It is recommended that you keep this service running.

data.Name: HvHost
data.Description: Provides an interface for the Hyper-V hypervisor to provide per-partition performance counters to the host operating system.

data.Name: vmickvpexchange
data.Description: Provides a mechanism to exchange data between the virtual machine and the operating system running on the physical computer.

data.Name: vmicguestinterface
data.Description: Provides an interface for the Hyper-V host to interact with specific services running inside the virtual machine.

data.Name: vmicshutdown
data.Description: Provides a mechanism to shut down the operating system of this virtual machine from the management interfaces on the physical computer.

data.Name: vmicheartbeat
data.Description: Monitors the state of this virtual machine by reporting a heartbeat at regular intervals. This service helps you identify running virtual machines that have stopped responding.

data.Name: vmcompute
data.Description: Provides support for running Windows Containers and Virtual Machines.

data.Name: vmicvmsession
data.Description: Provides a mechanism to manage virtual machine with PowerShell via VM session without a virtual network.

data.Name: vmicrdv
data.Description: Provides a platform for communication between the virtual machine and the operating system running on the physical computer.

data.Name: vmictimesync
data.Description: Synchronizes the system time of this virtual machine with the system time of the physical computer.

data.Name: vmicvss
data.Description: Coordinates the communications that are required to use Volume Shadow Copy Service to back up applications and data on this virtual machine from the operating system on the physical computer.

data.Name: IKEEXT
data.DisplayName: IKE and AuthIP IPsec Keying Modules
data.Description: The IKEEXT service hosts the Internet Key Exchange (IKE) and Authenticated Internet Protocol (AuthIP) keying modules. These keying modules are used for authentication and key exchange in Internet Protocol security (IPsec). Stopping or disabling the IKEEXT service will disable IKE and AuthIP key exchange with peer computers. IPsec is typically configured to use IKE or AuthIP; therefore, stopping or disabling the IKEEXT service might result in an IPsec failure and might compromise the security of the system. It is strongly recommended that you have the IKEEXT service running.

data.Name: Intel Network Helper Service
data.Description: Intel Network Helper Service

data.Name: cplspcon
data.Description: Intel(R) Content Protection HDCP Service - enables communication with Content Protection HDCP HW

data.Name: jhi_service
data.DisplayName: Intel(R) Dynamic Application Loader Host Interface Service
data.Description: Intel(R) Dynamic Application Loader Host Interface Service - Allows applications to access the local Intel (R) DAL

data.Name: dptftcs
data.DisplayName: Intel(R) Dynamic Tuning Technology Telemetry Service
data.Description: Intel(R) Dynamic Tuning Technology Telemetry Service

data.Name: XtuService
data.Description: Service used for XTU and XTU SDK applications

data.Name: ipfsvc
data.Description: Intel(R) Innovation Platform Framework Service

data.Name: WMIRegistrationService
data.DisplayName: Intel(R) Management Engine WMI Provider Registration
data.Description: Intel(R) Management Engine WMI Provider Registration Service

data.Name: RstMwService
data.Description: RPC endpoint service which allows communication between driver and Windows Store Application

data.Name: IntelGraphicsSoftwareService
data.Description: Intelr Graphics Software

data.Name: PIEServiceNew
data.Description: This service is part of the Intelr PROSet/Wireless WiFi Software

data.Name: SharedAccess
data.Description: Provides network address translation, addressing, name resolution and/or intrusion prevention services for a home or small office network.

data.Name: InventorySvc
data.Description: This service performs background system inventory, compatibility appraisal, and maintenance used by numerous system components.

data.Name: iphlpsvc
data.Description: Provides tunnel connectivity using IPv6 transition technologies (6to4, ISATAP, Port Proxy, and Teredo), and IP-HTTPS. If this service is stopped, the computer will not have the enhanced connectivity benefits that these technologies offer.

data.Name: IpxlatCfgSvc
data.Description: Configures and enables translation from v4 to v6 and vice versa

data.Name: PolicyAgent
data.Description: Internet Protocol security (IPsec) supports network-level peer authentication, data origin authentication, data integrity, data confidentiality (encryption), and replay protection.  This service enforces IPsec policies created through the IP Security Policies snap-in or the command-line tool "netsh ipsec".  If you stop this service, you may experience network connectivity issues if your policy requires that connections use IPsec.  Also,remote management of Windows Defender Firewall is not available when this service is stopped.

data.Name: LocalKdc
data.Description: This service enables users to log on to the local machine using the Kerberos authentication protocol. If this service is stopped, users will be unable to log on to the local machine. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: Killer Analytics Service
data.Description: Killer Analytics Service

data.Name: KNDBWM
data.Description: This service dynamically manages bandwidth settings based on detection of internet traffic congestion.

data.Name: Killer Network Service
data.Description: Killer Network Service provides the Killer software functionality

data.Name: Killer Provider Data Helper Service
data.Description: Killer Provider Data Helper Service

data.Name: KAPSService
data.Description: Offers Wifi related suggestions to the Killer Network Service

data.Name: KtmRm
data.Description: Coordinates transactions between the Distributed Transaction Coordinator (MSDTC) and the Kernel Transaction Manager (KTM). If it is not needed, it is recommended that this service remain stopped. If it is needed, both MSDTC and KTM will start this service automatically. If this service is disabled, any MSDTC transaction interacting with a Kernel Resource Manager will fail and any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: LxpSvc
data.Description: Provides infrastructure support for deploying and configuring localized Windows resources. This service is started on demand and, if disabled, additional Windows languages will not be deployed to the system, and Windows may not function properly.

data.Name: lltdsvc
data.Description: Creates a Network Map, consisting of PC and device topology (connectivity) information, and metadata describing each PC and device.  If this service is disabled, the Network Map will not function properly.

data.Name: wlpasvc
data.Description: This service provides profile management for subscriber identity modules

data.Name: mc-fw-host
data.Description: McAfee Framework Host Service

data.Name: McAfee WebAdvisor
data.Description: McAfee WebAdvisor Service

data.Name: McpManagementService
data.Description: Universal Print Management Service

data.Name: MessagingService_14814b
data.Description: Service supporting text messaging and related functionality.

data.Name: wlidsvc
data.Description: Enables user sign-in through Microsoft account identity services. If this service is stopped, users will not be able to logon to the computer with their Microsoft account.

data.Name: AppVClient
data.Description: Manages App-V users and virtual applications

data.Name: cloudidsvc
data.Description: Supports integrations with Microsoft cloud identity services.  If disabled, tenant restrictions will not be enforced properly.

data.Name: MicrosoftCopilotElevationService
data.Description: Provides elevated privileges for Copilot.

data.Name: WdNisSvc
data.DisplayName: Microsoft Defender Antivirus Network Inspection Service
data.Description: Helps guard against intrusion attempts targeting known and newly discovered vulnerabilities in network protocols

data.Name: WinDefend
data.Description: Helps protect users from malware and other potentially unwanted software

info.what: Real-time malware protection. Never disable.

data.Name: MDCoreSvc
data.Description: Monitors the availability, health, and performance of various security components

data.Name: MicrosoftEdgeElevationService
data.Description: Provides elevated privileges for Microsoft Edge.

data.Name: edgeupdate
data.Description: Keeps your Microsoft software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your Microsoft software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no Microsoft software using it.

data.Name: edgeupdatem
data.Description: Keeps your Microsoft software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your Microsoft software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no Microsoft software using it.

data.Name: MSiSCSI
data.Description: Manages Internet SCSI (iSCSI) sessions from this computer to remote iSCSI target devices. If this service is stopped, this computer will not be able to login or access iSCSI targets. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: MsKeyboardFilter
data.Description: Controls keystroke filtering and mapping

data.Name: ClickToRunSvc
data.Description: ?Manages resource coordination, background streaming, and system integration of Microsoft 365 and Office products and their related updates. This service is required to run during the use of any Microsoft 365 and Office program, during initial streaming installation and all subsequent updates.?

data.Name: NgcSvc
data.Description: Provides process isolation for cryptographic keys used to authenticate to a user's associated identity providers. If this service is disabled, all uses and management of these keys will not be available, which includes machine logon and single-sign on for apps and websites. This service starts and stops automatically. It is recommended that you do not reconfigure this service.

data.Name: NgcCtnrSvc
data.Description: Manages local user identity keys used to authenticate user to identity providers as well as TPM virtual smart cards. If this service is disabled, local user identity keys and TPM virtual smart cards will not be accessible. It is recommended that you do not reconfigure this service.

data.Name: PCManager Service Store
data.Description: Microsoft PC Manager Service

data.Name: swprv
data.Description: Manages software-based volume shadow copies taken by the Volume Shadow Copy service. If this service is stopped, software-based volume shadow copies cannot be managed. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: smphost
data.Description: Host service for the Microsoft Storage Spaces management provider. If this service is stopped or disabled, Storage Spaces cannot be managed.

data.Name: InstallService
data.Description: Provides infrastructure support for the Microsoft Store.  This service is started on demand and if disabled then installations will not function properly.

data.Name: wuqisvc
data.Description: A Microsoft service producing summary facts and insights related to usage and quality of experience. Facts are used to automate on-device self-healing and other optional workflows, such as Personalized offers.

data.Name: SmsRouter
data.Description: Routes messages based on rules to appropriate clients.

data.Name: McmSvc
data.Description: This service provides profile management for mobile connectivity modules

data.Name: NaturalAuthentication
data.Description: Signal aggregator service, that evaluates signals based on time, network, geolocation, bluetooth and cdf factors. Supported features are Device Unlock, Dynamic Lock and Dynamo MDM policies

data.Name: NetTcpPortSharing
data.Description: Provides ability to share TCP ports over the net.tcp protocol.

data.Name: Netlogon
data.Description: Maintains a secure channel between this computer and the domain controller for authenticating users and services. If this service is stopped, the computer may not authenticate users and services and the domain controller cannot register DNS records. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: NcdAutoSetup
data.Description: Network Connected Devices Auto-Setup service monitors and installs qualified devices that connect to a qualified network. Stopping or disabling this service will prevent Windows from discovering and installing qualified network connected devices automatically. Users can still manually add network connected devices to a PC through the user interface.

data.Name: NcbService
data.Description: Brokers connections that allow packaged Microsoft Store apps to receive notifications from the internet.

data.Name: Netman
data.Description: Manages objects in the Network and Dial-Up Connections folder, in which you can view both local area network and remote connections.

data.Name: NcaSvc
data.Description: Provides DirectAccess status notification for UI components

data.Name: netprofm
data.Description: Identifies the networks to which the computer has connected, collects and stores properties for these networks, and notifies applications when these properties change.

data.Name: NlaSvc
data.Description: Collects and stores configuration information for the network and notifies programs when this information is modified. If this service is stopped, configuration information might be unavailable. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: nsi
data.Description: This service delivers network notifications (e.g. interface addition/deleting etc) to user mode clients. Stopping this service will cause loss of network connectivity. If this service is disabled, any other services that explicitly depend on this service will fail to start.

data.Name: nvagent
data.Description: Provides network virtualization services.

data.Name: NPSMSvc_14814b
data.Description: This service hosts the Now Playing Session Manager used for Media Scenarios

data.Name: NVDisplay.ContainerLocalSystem
data.Description: Container service for NVIDIA root features

data.Name: FvSvc
data.Description: NVIDIA FrameView SDK service

data.Name: NvContainerLocalSystem
data.Description: Container service for NVIDIA root features

data.Name: CscService
data.Description: The Offline Files service performs maintenance activities on the Offline Files cache, responds to user logon and logoff events, implements the internals of the public API, and dispatches interesting events to those interested in Offline Files activities and changes in cache state.

data.Name: OneDrive Updater Service
data.Description: Keeps your OneDrive up to date.

data.Name: OneSyncSvc_14814b
data.Description: This service synchronizes mail, contacts, calendar and various other user data. Mail and other applications dependent on this functionality will not work properly when this service is not running.

data.Name: ssh-agent
data.Description: Agent to hold private keys used for public key authentication.

data.Name: defragsvc
data.Description: Helps the computer run more efficiently by optimizing files on storage drives.

data.Name: P9RdrService_14814b
data.Description: Enables trigger-starting plan9 file servers.

data.Name: WpcMonSvc
data.Description: Enforces parental controls for child accounts in Windows. If this service is stopped or disabled, parental controls may not be enforced.

data.Name: SEMgrSvc
data.Description: Manages payments and Near Field Communication (NFC) based secure elements.

data.Name: PenService_14814b
data.Description: Pen Service

data.Name: PerfHost
data.Description: Enables remote users and 64-bit processes to query performance counters provided by 32-bit DLLs. If this service is stopped, only local users and 32-bit processes will be able to query performance counters provided by 32-bit DLLs.

data.Name: pla
data.Description: Performance Logs and Alerts Collects performance data from local or remote computers based on preconfigured schedule parameters, then writes the data to a log or triggers an alert. If this service is stopped, performance information will not be collected. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: PhoneSvc
data.Description: Manages the telephony state on the device

data.Name: PimIndexMaintenanceSvc_14814b
data.Description: Indexes contact data for fast contact searching. If you stop or disable this service, contacts might be missing from your search results.

data.Name: PlexUpdateService
data.Description: Enables automatic updates of the Plex Media Server

data.Name: PlugPlay
data.Description: Enables a computer to recognize and adapt to hardware changes with little or no user input. Stopping or disabling this service will result in system instability.

data.Name: WPDBusEnum
data.Description: Enforces group policy for removable mass-storage devices. Enables applications such as Windows Media Player and Image Import Wizard to transfer and synchronize content using removable mass-storage devices.

data.Name: Power
data.Description: Manages power policy and power policy notification delivery.

data.Name: PrintDeviceConfigurationService
data.Description: The Print Device Configuration Service manages the installation of IPP and UP printers. If this service is stopped, any printer installations that are in-progress may be canceled.

data.Name: Spooler
data.Description: This service spools print jobs and handles interaction with the printer.  If you turn off this service, you won't be able to print or see your printers.

info.what: Manages print jobs. Safe to disable if you never print.

data.Name: PrintNotify
data.Description: This service opens custom printer dialog boxes and handles notifications from a remote print server or a printer. If you turn off this service, you won't be able to see printer extensions or notifications.

data.Name: PrintScanBrokerService
data.Description: Provides support for secure privileged operations needed by low priv spooler.

data.Name: PrintWorkflowUserSvc_14814b
data.Description: Provides support for Print Workflow applications. If you turn off this service, you may not be able to print successfully.

data.Name: wercplsupport
data.Description: This service provides support for viewing, sending and deletion of system-level problem reports for the Problem Reports control panel.

data.Name: PcaSvc
data.Description: This service provides support for the Program Compatibility Assistant (PCA).  PCA monitors programs installed and run by the user and detects known compatibility problems. If this service is stopped, PCA will not function properly.

data.Name: QWAVE
data.Description: Quality Windows Audio Video Experience (qWave) is a networking platform for Audio Video (AV) streaming applications on IP home networks. qWave enhances AV streaming performance and reliability by ensuring network quality-of-service (QoS) for AV applications. It provides mechanisms for admission control, run time monitoring and enforcement, application feedback, and traffic prioritization.

data.Name: RmSvc
data.Description: Radio Management and Airplane Mode Service

data.Name: RtkAudioUniversalService
data.Description: Realtek Audio Universal Service

data.Name: TroubleshootingSvc
data.Description: Enables automatic mitigation for known problems by applying recommended troubleshooting. If stopped, your device will not get recommended troubleshooting for problems on your device.

data.Name: refsdedupsvc
data.Description: ReFS data deduplication and compression service to track file changes and run scheduled optimization jobs.

data.Name: RasAuto
data.Description: Creates a connection to a remote network whenever a program references a remote DNS or NetBIOS name or address.

data.Name: RasMan
data.Description: Manages dial-up and virtual private network (VPN) connections from this computer to the Internet or other remote networks. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: SessionEnv
data.Description: Remote Desktop Configuration service (RDCS) is responsible for all Remote Desktop Services and Remote Desktop related configuration and session maintenance activities that require SYSTEM context. These include per-session temporary folders, RD themes, and RD certificates.

data.Name: TermService
data.Description: Allows users to connect interactively to a remote computer. Remote Desktop and Remote Desktop Session Host Server depend on this service.  To prevent remote use of this computer, clear the checkboxes on the Remote tab of the System properties control panel item.

info.what: Enables Remote Desktop connections to this PC. Disable if you don't use RDP.

data.Name: UmRdpService
data.DisplayName: Remote Desktop Services UserMode Port Redirector
data.Description: Allows the redirection of Printers/Drives/Ports for RDP connections

data.Name: RpcSs
data.Description: The RPCSS service is the Service Control Manager for COM and DCOM servers. It performs object activations requests, object exporter resolutions and distributed garbage collection for COM and DCOM servers. If this service is stopped or disabled, programs using COM or DCOM will not function properly. It is strongly recommended that you have the RPCSS service running.

info.what: Core Windows RPC subsystem. Never disable — system will fail to boot.

data.Name: RpcLocator
data.Description: In Windows 2003 and earlier versions of Windows, the Remote Procedure Call (RPC) Locator service manages the RPC name service database. In Windows Vista and later versions of Windows, this service does not provide any functionality and is present for application compatibility.

data.Name: RemoteRegistry
data.Description: Enables remote users to modify registry settings on this computer. If this service is stopped, the registry can be modified only by users on this computer. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Allows remote editing of registry. Disable for security unless specifically needed.

data.Name: RetailDemo
data.Description: The Retail Demo service controls device activity while the device is in retail demo mode.

data.Name: RemoteAccess
data.Description: Offers routing services to businesses in local area and wide area network environments.

data.Name: RpcEptMapper
data.Description: Resolves RPC interfaces identifiers to transport endpoints. If this service is stopped or disabled, programs using Remote Procedure Call (RPC) services will not function properly.

data.Name: SamsungMagicianSVC
data.Description: Samsung Magician is designed to help users easily manage their Samsung SSD(s) and conveniently update the firmware as well as optimize their SSD(s) for performance and reliability.

data.Name: seclogon
data.Description: Enables starting processes under alternate credentials. If this service is stopped, this type of logon access will be unavailable. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Allows running programs as a different user (Run As). Safe to disable if unused.

data.Name: SstpSvc
data.Description: Provides support for the Secure Socket Tunneling Protocol (SSTP) to connect to remote computers using VPN. If this service is disabled, users will not be able to use SSTP to access remote servers.

data.Name: SamSs
data.Description: The startup of this service signals other services that the Security Accounts Manager (SAM) is ready to accept requests.  Disabling this service will prevent other services in the system from being notified when the SAM is ready, which may in turn cause those services to fail to start correctly. This service should not be disabled.

data.Name: wscsvc
data.Description: The WSCSVC (Windows Security Center) service monitors and reports security health settings on the computer.  The health settings include firewall (on/off), antivirus (on/off/out of date), antispyware (on/off/out of date), Windows Update (automatically/manually download and install updates), User Account Control (on/off), and Internet settings (recommended/not recommended). The service provides COM APIs for independent software vendors to register and record the state of their products to the Security Center service.  The Security and Maintenance UI uses the service to provide systray alerts and a graphical view of the security health states in the Security and Maintenance control panel.  Network Access Protection (NAP) uses the service to report the security health states of clients to the NAP Network Policy Server to make network quarantine decisions.  The service also has a public API that allows external consumers to programmatically retrieve the aggregated security health state of the system.

data.Name: SensorDataService
data.Description: Delivers data from a variety of sensors

data.Name: SensrSvc
data.Description: Monitors various sensors in order to expose data and adapt to system and user state.  If this service is stopped or disabled, the display brightness will not adapt to lighting conditions. Stopping this service may affect other system functionality and features as well.

data.Name: SensorService
data.Description: A service for sensors that manages different sensors' functionality. Manages Simple Device Orientation (SDO) and History for sensors. Loads the SDO sensor that reports device orientation changes.  If this service is stopped or disabled, the SDO sensor will not be loaded and so auto-rotation will not occur. History collection from Sensors will also be stopped.

data.Name: LanmanServer
data.Description: Supports file, print, and named-pipe sharing over the network for this computer. If this service is stopped, these functions will be unavailable. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Enables file and printer sharing from this PC. Safe to disable if not sharing.

data.Name: shpamsvc
data.Description: Manages profiles and accounts on a SharedPC configured device

data.Name: ShellHWDetection
data.Description: Provides notifications for AutoPlay hardware events.

data.Name: SCardSvr
data.Description: Manages access to smart cards read by this computer. If this service is stopped, this computer will be unable to read smart cards. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: ScDeviceEnum
data.Description: Creates software device nodes for all smart card readers accessible to a given session. If this service is disabled, WinRT APIs will not be able to enumerate smart card readers.

data.Name: SCPolicySvc
data.Description: Allows the system to be configured to lock the user desktop upon smart card removal.

data.Name: SNMPTrap
data.Description: Receives trap messages generated by local or remote Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) agents and forwards the messages to SNMP management programs running on this computer. If this service is stopped, SNMP-based programs on this computer will not receive SNMP trap messages. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: sppsvc
data.Description: Enables the download, installation and enforcement of digital licenses for Windows and Windows applications. If the service is disabled, the operating system and licensed applications may run in a notification mode. It is strongly recommended that you not disable the Software Protection service.

data.Name: svsvc
data.Description: Verifies potential file system corruptions.

data.Name: SSDPSRV
data.Description: Discovers networked devices and services that use the SSDP discovery protocol, such as UPnP devices. Also announces SSDP devices and services running on the local computer. If this service is stopped, SSDP-based devices will not be discovered. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Discovers UPnP devices on the network. Safe to disable with UPnP Host.

data.Name: StateRepository
data.Description: Provides required infrastructure support for the application model.

data.Name: WiaRpc
data.Description: Launches applications associated with still image acquisition events.

data.Name: StorSvc
data.Description: Provides enabling services for storage settings and external storage expansion

data.Name: TieringEngineService
data.Description: Optimizes the placement of data in storage tiers on all tiered storage spaces in the system.

data.Name: SysMain
data.Description: Maintains and improves system performance over time.

info.what: Pre-loads frequently used apps into RAM. On SSDs it adds little value.

data.Name: SENS
data.Description: Monitors system events and notifies subscribers to COM+ Event System of these events.

data.Name: SystemEventsBroker
data.Description: Coordinates execution of background work for WinRT application. If this service is stopped or disabled, then background work might not be triggered.

data.Name: Schedule
data.Description: Enables a user to configure and schedule automated tasks on this computer. The service also hosts multiple Windows system-critical tasks. If this service is stopped or disabled, these tasks will not be run at their scheduled times. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Runs scheduled tasks — including WinDesktopMgr at login. Do not disable.

data.Name: lmhosts
data.Description: Provides support for the NetBIOS over TCP/IP (NetBT) service and NetBIOS name resolution for clients on the network, therefore enabling users to share files, print, and log on to the network. If this service is stopped, these functions might be unavailable. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Supports old NetBIOS network name resolution. Rarely needed on modern networks.

data.Name: TapiSrv
data.Description: Provides Telephony API (TAPI) support for programs that control telephony devices on the local computer and, through the LAN, on servers that are also running the service.

data.Name: TextInputManagementService
data.Description: Enables text input, expressive input, touch keyboard, handwriting, and IMEs.

data.Name: Themes
data.Description: Provides user experience theme management.

info.what: Applies visual themes to the Windows UI. Disabling reverts to a basic look.

data.Name: TimeBrokerSvc
data.Description: Coordinates execution of background work for WinRT application. If this service is stopped or disabled, then background work might not be triggered.

data.Name: UdkUserSvc_14814b
data.Description: Shell components service

data.Name: UnistoreSvc_14814b
data.Description: Handles storage of structured user data, including contact info, calendars, messages, and other content. If you stop or disable this service, apps that use this data might not work correctly.

data.Name: UsoSvc
data.Description: Manages Windows Updates. If stopped, your devices will not be able to download and install the latest updates.

data.Name: upnphost
data.Description: Allows UPnP devices to be hosted on this computer. If this service is stopped, any hosted UPnP devices will stop functioning and no additional hosted devices can be added. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Hosts UPnP devices. Safe to disable if you don't use UPnP sharing.

data.Name: UevAgentService
data.Description: Provides support for application and OS settings roaming

data.Name: UserManager
data.Description: User Manager provides the runtime components required for multi-user interaction.  If this service is stopped, some applications may not operate correctly.

data.Name: ProfSvc
data.Description: This service is responsible for loading and unloading user profiles. If this service is stopped or disabled, users will no longer be able to successfully sign in or sign out, apps might have problems getting to users' data, and components registered to receive profile event notifications won't receive them.

data.Name: UserDataSvc_14814b
data.Description: Provides apps access to structured user data, including contact info, calendars, messages, and other content. If you stop or disable this service, apps that use this data might not work correctly.

data.Name: vds
data.Description: Provides management services for disks, volumes, file systems, and storage arrays.

data.Name: VSS
data.Description: Manages and implements Volume Shadow Copies used for backup and other purposes. If this service is stopped, shadow copies will be unavailable for backup and the backup may fail. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: WalletService
data.Description: Hosts objects used by clients of the wallet

data.Name: WarpJITSvc
data.Description: Enables JIT compilation support in d3d10warp.dll for processes in which code generation is disabled.

data.Name: WavesSysSvc
data.Description: Waves Audio global services

data.Name: WavesAudioService
data.Description: Waves Audio universal global services

data.Name: TokenBroker
data.Description: This service is used by Web Account Manager to provide single-sign-on to apps and services.

data.Name: webthreatdefsvc
data.Description: Web Threat Defense Endpoint Service helps protect your computer by identifying unauthorized entities attempting to gain access to user credentials

data.Name: WebClient
data.Description: Enables Windows-based programs to create, access, and modify Internet-based files. If this service is stopped, these functions will not be available. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: webthreatdefusersvc_14814b
data.Description: Web Threat Defense User Service helps protect your computer by warning the user when unauthorized entities attempt to gain access to their credentials

data.Name: WFDSConMgrSvc
data.DisplayName: Wi-Fi Direct Services Connection Manager Service
data.Description: Manages connections to wireless services, including wireless display and docking.

data.Name: Audiosrv
data.Description: Manages audio for Windows-based programs.  If this service is stopped, audio devices and effects will not function properly.  If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start

data.Name: AudioEndpointBuilder
data.Description: Manages audio devices for the Windows Audio service.  If this service is stopped, audio devices and effects will not function properly.  If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start

data.Name: SDRSVC
data.Description: Provides Windows Backup and Restore capabilities.

data.Name: WbioSrvc
data.Description: The Windows biometric service gives client applications the ability to capture, compare, manipulate, and store biometric data without gaining direct access to any biometric hardware or samples. The service is hosted in a privileged SVCHOST process.

data.Name: FrameServer
data.Description: Enables multiple clients to access video frames from camera devices.

data.Name: FrameServerMonitor
data.Description: Monitors the health and state for the Windows Camera Frame Server service.

data.Name: wcncsvc
data.Description: WCNCSVC hosts the Windows Connect Now Configuration which is Microsoft's Implementation of Wireless Protected Setup (WPS) protocol. This is used to configure Wireless LAN settings for an Access Point (AP) or a Wireless Device. The service is started programmatically as needed.

data.Name: Wcmsvc
data.Description: Makes automatic connect and disconnect decisions based on the network connectivity options currently available to the PC and enables management of network connectivity based on Group Policy settings.

data.Name: Sense
data.DisplayName: Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection Service
data.Description: Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection service helps protect against advanced threats by monitoring and reporting security events that happen on the computer.

data.Name: mpssvc
data.Description: Windows Defender Firewall helps protect your computer by preventing unauthorized users from gaining access to your computer through the Internet or a network.

info.what: Network firewall. Never disable.

data.Name: WEPHOSTSVC
data.Description: Windows Encryption Provider Host Service brokers encryption related functionalities from 3rd Party Encryption Providers to processes that need to evaluate and apply EAS policies. Stopping this will compromise EAS compliancy checks that have been established by the connected Mail Accounts

data.Name: WerSvc
data.Description: Allows errors to be reported when programs stop working or responding and allows existing solutions to be delivered. Also allows logs to be generated for diagnostic and repair services. If this service is stopped, error reporting might not work correctly and results of diagnostic services and repairs might not be displayed.

info.what: Sends crash reports to Microsoft. Safe to disable for privacy.

data.Name: Wecsvc
data.Description: This service manages persistent subscriptions to events from remote sources that support WS-Management protocol. This includes Windows Vista event logs, hardware and IPMI-enabled event sources. The service stores forwarded events in a local Event Log. If this service is stopped or disabled event subscriptions cannot be created and forwarded events cannot be accepted.

data.Name: EventLog
data.Description: This service manages events and event logs. It supports logging events, querying events, subscribing to events, archiving event logs, and managing event metadata. It can display events in both XML and plain text format. Stopping this service may compromise security and reliability of the system.

info.what: Records system events. Required for BSOD Dashboard and Event Log tab. Never disable.

data.Name: FontCache
data.Description: Optimizes performance of applications by caching commonly used font data. Applications will start this service if it is not already running. It can be disabled, though doing so will degrade application performance.

data.Name: whesvc
data.Description: Monitors the device for a better user experience

data.Name: StiSvc
data.Description: Provides image acquisition services for scanners and cameras

data.Name: wisvc
data.Description: Provides infrastructure support for the Windows Insider Program. This service must remain enabled for the Windows Insider Program to work.

data.Name: msiserver
data.Description: Adds, modifies, and removes applications provided as a Windows Installer (*.msi, *.msp) package. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: LicenseManager
data.Description: Provides infrastructure support for the Microsoft Store.  This service is started on demand and if disabled then content acquired through the Microsoft Store will not function properly.

data.Name: Winmgmt
data.Description: Provides a common interface and object model to access management information about operating system, devices, applications and services. If this service is stopped, most Windows-based software will not function properly. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: WManSvc
data.Description: Performs management including Provisioning and Enrollment activities

data.Name: WMPNetworkSvc
data.DisplayName: Windows Media Player Network Sharing Service
data.Description: Shares Windows Media Player libraries to other networked players and media devices using Universal Plug and Play

info.what: Shares media libraries over the network. Safe to disable if unused.

data.Name: midisrv
data.Description: Windows MIDI Services core service for MIDI 1.0 and MIDI 2.0 message routing and device enumeration

data.Name: icssvc
data.Description: Provides the ability to share a cellular data connection with another device.

data.Name: TrustedInstaller
data.Description: Enables installation, modification, and removal of Windows updates and optional components. If this service is disabled, install or uninstall of Windows updates might fail for this computer.

data.Name: perceptionsimulation
data.Description: Enables spatial perception simulation, virtual camera management and spatial input simulation.

data.Name: WpnService
data.Description: This service runs in session 0 and hosts the notification platform and connection provider which handles the connection between the device and WNS server.

data.Name: PushToInstall
data.Description: Provides infrastructure support for the Microsoft Store.  This service is started automatically and if disabled then remote installations will not function properly.

data.Name: WinRM
data.Description: Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service implements the WS-Management protocol for remote management. WS-Management is a standard web services protocol used for remote software and hardware management. The WinRM service listens on the network for WS-Management requests and processes them. The WinRM Service needs to be configured with a listener using winrm.cmd command line tool or through Group Policy in order for it to listen over the network. The WinRM service provides access to WMI data and enables event collection. Event collection and subscription to events require that the service is running. WinRM messages use HTTP and HTTPS as transports. The WinRM service does not depend on IIS but is preconfigured to share a port with IIS on the same machine.  The WinRM service reserves the /wsman URL prefix. To prevent conflicts with IIS, administrators should ensure that any websites hosted on IIS do not use the /wsman URL prefix.

data.Name: WSearch
data.Description: Provides content indexing, property caching, and search results for files, e-mail, and other content.

info.what: Indexes files for fast search in Explorer. Disabling saves RAM but slows file search.

data.Name: SecurityHealthService
data.Description: Windows Security Service handles unified device protection and health information

data.Name: W32Time
data.Description: Maintains date and time synchronization on all clients and servers in the network. If this service is stopped, date and time synchronization will be unavailable. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

data.Name: wuauserv
data.Description: Enables the detection, download, and installation of updates for Windows and other programs. If this service is disabled, users of this computer will not be able to use Windows Update or its automatic updating feature, and programs will not be able to use the Windows Update Agent (WUA) API.

info.what: Downloads and installs Windows updates. Required for system security.

data.Name: ApxSvc
data.DisplayName: Windows Virtual Audio Device Proxy Service
data.Description: Manages virtual audio devices for Windows-based programs.  If this service is stopped, virtual audio devices will not function properly.  If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start

data.Name: WinHttpAutoProxySvc
data.Description: WinHTTP implements the client HTTP stack and provides developers with a Win32 API and COM Automation component for sending HTTP requests and receiving responses. In addition, WinHTTP provides support for auto-discovering a proxy configuration via its implementation of the Web Proxy Auto-Discovery (WPAD) protocol.

data.Name: dot3svc
data.Description: The Wired AutoConfig (DOT3SVC) service is responsible for performing IEEE 802.1X authentication on Ethernet interfaces. If your current wired network deployment enforces 802.1X authentication, the DOT3SVC service should be configured to run for establishing Layer 2 connectivity and/or providing access to network resources. Wired networks that do not enforce 802.1X authentication are unaffected by the DOT3SVC service.

data.Name: WlanSvc
data.Description: The WLANSVC service provides the logic required to configure, discover, connect to, and disconnect from a wireless local area network (WLAN) as defined by IEEE 802.11 standards. It also contains the logic to turn your computer into a software access point so that other devices or computers can connect to your computer wirelessly using a WLAN adapter that can support this. Stopping or disabling the WLANSVC service will make all WLAN adapters on your computer inaccessible from the Windows networking UI. It is strongly recommended that you have the WLANSVC service running if your computer has a WLAN adapter.

data.Name: wmiApSrv
data.Description: Provides performance library information from Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) providers to clients on the network. This service only runs when Performance Data Helper is activated.

data.Name: workfolderssvc
data.Description: This service syncs files with the Work Folders server, enabling you to use the files on any of the PCs and devices on which you've set up Work Folders.

data.Name: LanmanWorkstation
data.Description: Creates and maintains client network connections to remote servers using the SMB protocol. If this service is stopped, these connections will be unavailable. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

info.what: Allows connecting to shared network files and printers. Disable only if fully isolated.

data.Name: WpnUserService_14814b
data.Description: This service hosts Windows notification platform which provides support for local and push notifications. Supported notifications are tile, toast and raw.

data.Name: WSAIFabricSvc
data.Description: Provides support to communicate with AIFabric in Local service context over COM.

data.Name: WSLService
data.Description: WSL Service

data.Name: WwanSvc
data.Description: This service manages mobile broadband (GSM & CDMA) data card/embedded module adapters and connections by auto-configuring the networks. It is strongly recommended that this service be kept running for best user experience of mobile broadband devices.

data.Name: XboxGipSvc
data.Description: This service manages connected Xbox Accessories.

data.Name: XblAuthManager
data.Description: Provides authentication and authorization services for interacting with Xbox Live. If this service is stopped, some applications may not operate correctly.

info.what: Xbox Live authentication. Safe to disable if you don't use Xbox.

data.Name: XblGameSave
data.Description: This service syncs save data for Xbox Live save enabled games.  If this service is stopped, game save data will not upload to or download from Xbox Live.

info.what: Syncs Xbox game saves to the cloud. Safe to disable if unused.

data.Name: XboxNetApiSvc
data.Description: This service supports the Windows.Networking.XboxLive application programming interface.

info.what: Xbox Live multiplayer networking. Safe to disable if unused.

data.Name: ZoomCptServiceForVDIPluginMgmt
data.DisplayName: Zoom Service for VDI Plugin Management
data.Description: Provides Admin Privileges for VDI Plugin Installation.
